meaning: adj.有尖牙的,有牙根的,有毒牙的
examples: The piercing wind fanged his ears.
Strange as it seems, a de-fanged, steady-state version of the current settlement might offer the best prospect humankind has ever had of avoiding collapse.
Then it wants to license those innovations to the world (and pursue infringers with razor-fanged determination).
"Monsters University" is set on a leafy campus with brick buildings and cobblestone paths, "monsterized" to include fanged archways and spiked domes.
